learning_outcomes.description: Locate, synthesize, and critically assess contemporary mathematical and statistical research; identify relevant theories, methods, and open problems using advanced bibliographic tools and databases.

Formulate precise research questions, select or develop appropriate analytical, computational, or experimental techniques, and generate valid, reproducible results that withstand mathematical and statistical scrutiny.

Apply advanced mathematical reasoning, statistical inference, and efficient implementation skills (e.g., coding, simulation, numerical methods) to solve complex problems, justify conclusions, and assess limitations.
Deliver a well-structured seminar that informs and engages a general mathematical audience, employing clear logic, illustrative examples, and effective data visualisation.
Produce a thesis that is coherent, rigorously argued, and appropriately referenced, situating the work within the broader discipline and comparing alternative approaches.

Articulate and justify methodological choices and findings in a formal defense; respond thoughtfully to critique, reflect on the project's significance and limitations, and propose meaningful directions for future research.

assessments.task_description:
The progress report should contain

(*) a substantially complete literature review,

(*) a draft outline of the thesis.  (e.g. chapters and titles), and

(*) a realistic schedule of tasks to be completed in the remainder of the time.
assessments.submission_guidelines: An electronic version is required, preferably in PDF format. The file is to be submitted to the Blackboard system.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
27/07/2026 - 30/10/2026

The precise date will be advised in due course.
assessments.task_description:
The aim of the seminar is to explain the project to a general mathematical audience that includes all your fellow graduate students and members of staff. At least the first half of the talk should be understandable by everyone. The last few minutes may be a little more technical and explain the project’s contributions to experts.

The seminar should last for about 20 minutes leaving about 5 minutes for questions.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The seminar should be video recorded. The video record could be submitted to the Blackboard system, or saved by the supervisor for one year.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: There is no formal page limit,  typically the thesis is up to 100 pages long.  No formatting  or referencing style is formally prescribed, but  formatting and referencing should be clear,  be used consistently, and be in accordance with the standard practices of the discipline.
assessments.submission_guidelines: An electronic version is required, preferably in PDF format. The file is to be submitted to the Blackboard system.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
16/11/2026 - 20/11/2026

The due date will be determined with your supervisor.
assessments.task_description: Students will be interviewed by a committee and required to answer questions regarding their research project.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The thesis defense should be video recorded and the recording must be uploaded to Blackboard within 48 hours of the defense. If no recording is uploaded then you will receive 0 marks for the defense.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Independent study

Students study and complete their thesis under the guidance of their advisor, and prepare for the presentation and defense.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
